titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Wing family correspondence
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 15657869.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday February 5, 2021 at 04:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract:
677 letters relating to the Wing family, 1859 March 7-1926 June 21, and undated. Also present are 96 pieces of ephemera, including invitations and calling cards, postcards, miscellaneous manuscript notes, and a photograph.
Individuals represented in the collection include George W. Wing; wife Mary Elizabeth Gould Wing; daughters Alice Earle Wing and Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace; sons Daniel Gould Wing and Thomas Ellwood Wing; and grandchildren Alice B. Brace, Lloyd DeWitt Brace, Roger Wing Brace, John R. Wing, and Winthrop B. Wing.
Letters primarily concern Wing family affairs taking place in Davenport, Iowa; Lincoln, Nebraska; West Newton, Massachusetts; and Scarsdale, New York.
35 letters concern George Wing's silver mining efforts in Montana. 16 letters by Elizabeth Wing to various family members document her 1891 trip to Pasadena, Riverside, and Long Beach, California. 18 letters by Alice Wing to various recipients document her work with the YMCA in France during World War I.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Michael Brown Rare Books, LLC on the William Robertson Coe Fund No. 3, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into 2 series: I. Correspondence, 1859-1926. II. Ephemera and other papers, 1862-1924.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: George W. Wing (1833-1918) of Barnstable, Massachusetts, married Mary Elizabeth Gould (1841-1895) of Providence, Rhode Island, in Scott, Iowa, on 1866 June 14. The couple had four children: Daniel Gould Wing (1868-1936), twins Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace (1870-1926) and Alice E. Wing (1870-1920), and Thomas Ellwood Wing (1872- ). The family were Quakers.
In the 1870s and early 1880s, George Wing worked as a bookkeeper in Davenport, Iowa. In 1884, the family moved to Lincoln, Nebraska, where they operated a small fruit and vegetable farm.

Daniel Gould Wing worked as a banker in West Newton, Massachusetts, and was eventually president of the First National Bank of Boston. He was married to Josephine Cabel (1867-1949), with whom he had one child, Katherine Cable Wing (1903-1989).

Elizabeth Brace attended the University of Nebraska in the late 1880s; in 1891, she began working at the school's library. Brace later graduated from the Library School of the Armour Institute of Chicago (the predecessor to the University of Illinois Library School) in 1897, and by 1901 was First Assistant at the University of Nebraska Library (a position she left in 1901 October). She was married to physicist Professor DeWitt B. Brace (1859-1906), with whom she had three children: Lloyd DeWitt Brace (1903-1986), Roger Wing Brace (1904-1966), and Alice B. Brace (1905- ). The family lived in Lincoln, Nebraska.

Alice Wing attended Alma College in Michigan from 1899 to 1901, and graduated from Illinois State Library School in 1904. She worked for a time as Acting Librarian at the Public Library at Albion, Michigan. During World War I, she served with the YMCA in France.

Thomas Wing lived in Scarsdale, New York, and was a senior partner of the law firm Wing, Russell, & Watterson. He was married to Katharine Weston (1871-1903) and later Helen Stanton Bouve (1874-1943), with whom he had two children: John R. Wing (approximately 1912- ) and Winthrop B. Wing (approximately 1913- ).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Wing Family Correspondence. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards.
For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Descriptive information for this collection is drawn largely from the dealer list.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 677 letters relating to the Wing family, 1859 March 7-1926 June 21, and undated. Also present are 96 pieces of ephemera, including invitations and calling cards, postcards, miscellaneous manuscript notes, and a photograph.

Individuals represented in the collection include George W. Wing; wife Mary Elizabeth Gould Wing; daughters Alice Earle Wing and Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace; sons Daniel Gould Wing and Thomas Ellwood Wing; and grandchildren Alice B. Brace, Lloyd DeWitt Brace, Roger Wing Brace, John R. Wing, and Winthrop B. Wing.
Letters primarily concern Wing family affairs taking place in Davenport, Iowa; Lincoln, Nebraska; West Newton, Massachusetts; and Scarsdale, New York.

35 letters concern George Wing's silver mining efforts in Montana. 16 letters by Elizabeth Wing to various family members document her 1891 trip to Pasadena, Riverside, and Long Beach, California. 18 letters by Alice Wing to various recipients document her work with the YMCA in France during World War I.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Wing Family Correspondence is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged alphabetically by family member. Letters within each file are grouped by correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains 677 letters (circa 3,634 pages--mostly handwritten--with 362 envelopes) relating to the Wing family.

Individuals represented in the series include George W. Wing; wife Mary Elizabeth Gould Wing; daughters Alice Earle Wing and Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace; sons Daniel Gould Wing and Thomas Ellwood Wing; and grandchildren Alice B. Brace, Lloyd DeWitt Brace, Roger Wing Brace, John R. Wing, and Winthrop B. Wing.
Letters primarily concern Wing family affairs taking place in Davenport, Iowa; Lincoln, Nebraska; West Newton, Massachusetts; and Scarsdale, New York.

35 letters concern George Wing's silver mining efforts in Montana. 16 letters by Elizabeth Wing to various family members document her 1891 trip to Pasadena, Riverside, and Long Beach, California. 18 letters by Alice Wing to various recipients document her work with the YMCA in France during World War I.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 19 letters from Alice B. Brace to mother Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace, brother Roger Wing Brace, and other relatives. Letters written from an unidentified location. Included are pieces of prose.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 letters from Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace to sister Alice Earle Wing, 13 to son Roger Wing Brace, 18 to father George W. Wing, 11 to mother Mary Elizabeth Gould Wing, and 3 to brothers Daniel Gould Wing and Thomas Ellwood Wing. Letters written form Lincoln, Nebraska; Long Beach, Pasadena, and Riverside, California; and Davenport, Iowa.

Includes 12 letters from social work educator Edith Abbbott; 7 from Abbott's sister, Gracie Abbott; 8 from Abba Willard Bowen; 8 from sister-in-law Josephine Cabel Wing; 18 from sister-in-law Katherine Weston Wing; and 14 from various relatives.
82 additional letters are from various acquaintances--35 of these relate to the death of Brace's husband, Professor DeWitt B. Brace.

did.unittitle: Letters to Alice Earle Wing (2 folders)
Letters to George W. Wing (3 folders)
Letters to Mary Elizabeth Gould Wing (2 folders)
Letters from Edith Abbott and Gracie Abbott (3 folders)
Letters from Josephine Cabel Wing and Katherine Weston Wing (4 folders)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 33 letters from Lloyd Brace to mother Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace, sister Alice B. Brace, brother Roger Wing Brace, and aunt Alice Earle Wing. Letters written from West Newton, Massachusetts, and the Culver Military Academy at Culver, Indiana.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 21 letters from Roger Wing Brace to mother Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace, sister Alice B. Brace, grandfather George W. Wing, and aunt Alice Earle Wing. Letters written from Andover and West Newton, Massachusetts, and Lake Winnipesaukee, New Hampshire.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 84 letters from Alice Earle Wing to sister Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace, 34 to father George W. Wing, 12 to mother Mary Elizabeth Gould Wing, and 15 to neices and nephews. Letters written from Bordeaux, Perigueux, and Paris, France, during World War I; Lincoln, Nebraska; Scarsdale, New York; and West Newton, Massachusetts.

did.unittitle: Letters to Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace (8 folders)
Letters to George W. Wing (3 folders)
Letters to Mary Elizabeth Gould Wing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 14 letters from Daniel Gould Wing to father George W. Wing and sister Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace. Letters written from Davenport, Iowa; Lincoln, Nebraska; and Boston, Massachusetts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 52 letters from George W. Wing to wife Mary Elizabeth Gould Wing, 19 to daughter Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace, and 6 to daughter Alice Earle Wing. Letters written from Buffalo, New York, and Scarsdale, New York; Providence, Rhode Island; Davenport, Iowa; Lincoln, Nebraska; and West Newton, Massachusetts.

Letters sent to Mary Wing from Argenta, Brown's Bridge, Dillon, Glendale, and Vipond, Montana, document George Wing's involvement with silver mining claims. Wing's brother, Robert T. Wing, was a pioneer in silver mining at Montana having gone there in 1863.
Includes 6 letters addressed to Wing.

did.unittitle: Letters to Mary Elizabeth Gould Wing (4 folders)
Letters to Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace (2 folders)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 letter from John R. Winthrop to aunt Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace. Letter written from an unidentified location.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 49 letters from Mary Elizabeth Gould Wing to husband George W. Wing, 28 to daughter Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace, and 3 to other relatives. Letters written from Davenport, Iowa, and Lincoln, Nebraska.

did.unittitle: Letters to George W. Wing (5 folders)
Letters to Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace (3 folders)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 31 letters from Thomas Ellwood Wing to sister Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace, 6 to father George W. Wing, and 4 to other relatives. Letters written from Davenport, Iowa; Lincoln, Nebraska; and Scarsdale, New York.

did.unittitle: Letters to Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace (3 folders)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 letter from Winthrop B. Wing to cousin Alice B. Brace, and 1 letter to aunt Elizabeth "Bessie" Russell Wing Brace. Letters written from an unidentified location.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 32 letters from various correspondents to members of the Wing and Brace families.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II contains 96 pieces of ephemera, including 13 invitations and calling cards addressed to either Mr. & Mrs. George Wing or Mr. & Mrs. DeWitt B. Brace, 7 postcards exchanged amongst the Wing family, 37 empty envelopes, 38 miscellaneous manuscript notes, and 1 photograph of an unidentified man.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged alphabetically by item type.
